expectations.text: HTML file generated: dashboard.html exists in outputs
expectations.evidence: File exists at outputs/dashboard.html, 8159 bytes, contains a complete HTML document with DOCTYPE, head, body, styles, and structured content (312 lines).

expectations.text: Three-column layout: Decided, In Discussion, Undecided columns are present
expectations.evidence: CSS grid with 'grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr' (line 47). Three <div class="column"> children with headings: 'Decided' (line 210), 'In Discussion' (line 246), 'Undecided' (line 262). Each column contains relevant cards.

expectations.text: Content fits viewport: No horizontal scrolling needed, max-width 960px
expectations.evidence: Line 183: <div style="max-width: 960px; margin: 0 auto; overflow-x: auto;">. Container is capped at 960px and centered. Grid columns use relative 1fr units. Responsive breakpoint at 700px collapses to single column.

expectations.text: Progress indicator: Shows decided count vs total (e.g., 3/7)
expectations.evidence:
Line 200-202:
7개 항목 중 3개 결정
(Korean for '3 decided out of 7 items'). Also includes a visual progress bar with 42.9%/14.3%/42.9% segments and labels '3 결정됨 / 1 논의 중 / 3 미결정'.

expectations.text: Correct classification: OAuth, PostgreSQL, React in Decided; API structure in In Discussion/Undecided
expectations.evidence: Decided column: '사용자 인증' with detail 'OAuth' (line 218), '데이터베이스' with detail 'PostgreSQL' (line 228), '프론트엔드' with detail 'React' (line 237). In Discussion column: 'API 구조' with detail 'REST vs GraphQL' (line 253). All classifications correct.

expectations.text: Dark theme applied: #0f0f1a background with status colors (green/yellow/gray)
expectations.evidence: body background: #0f0f1a (line 10). Green: #4ade80 used for .decided class (line 20), decided card borders, and progress bar segment. Yellow: #facc15 for .active class (line 21) and active card border/glow. Gray: #888/#6b7280 for .undecided class (line 22) and undecided card dashed borders.

claims.evidence: Counted 7 cards: 3 in Decided (사용자 인증, 데이터베이스, 프론트엔드), 1 in In Discussion (API 구조), 3 in Undecided (배포 전략, 모니터링, CI/CD 파이프라인). Matches state.md which lists 7 elements.

claims.claim: Progress bar percentages are mathematically correct
claims.evidence: 3/7 = 42.86% (shown as 42.9%), 1/7 = 14.29% (shown as 14.3%), 3/7 = 42.86% (shown as 42.9%). Sum = 100.1% due to rounding but visually acceptable.

claims.claim: Dependency relationships are shown on cards
claims.evidence: Each card includes a card-relation div showing dependencies, e.g., 'API 구조와 연동' for UserAuth, 'API 구조에서 의존' for Database and Frontend. These match the relations defined in state.md.
A 'decision-order' section at the bottom (lines 299-303) shows: API 구조 → CI/CD 파이프라인 → 배포 전략 → 모니터링. This is a reasonable topological ordering based on the dependency graph in state.md.

suggestions.assertion: Content fits viewport: No horizontal scrolling needed, max-width 960px
suggestions.reason: The wrapper div uses 'overflow-x: auto' which technically allows horizontal scrolling within the container. A stricter check would verify overflow-x is hidden or that no content exceeds the container width. However, in practice this is fine since grid columns are relative.

No assertion checks that the dashboard is valid/parseable HTML. A malformed document could pass all current checks. Consider adding a validation assertion or at least checking for DOCTYPE and closing tags.

No assertion checks the dependency relationships shown on cards. The dashboard includes dependency info (e.g., 'API 구조에서 의존') which is a key feature for planning visibility. Consider asserting that at least one dependency relationship is displayed.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover the main visual and structural requirements well. The classification check is strong since it requires correct content in the right columns. Minor gap: no assertion verifies dependency information or that the HTML is well-formed.
